differential_diagnoses: Tonsillar or oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced CT angiography (CTA) of the neck immediately to rule out pseudoaneurysm
Avoid any further invasive procedures or biopsies until vascular involvement is excluded
Consult interventional radiology for potential vascular embolization or stenting if a pseudoaneurysm is identified
Once vascular pathology is ruled out, proceed with surgical biopsy or definitive resection for histopathological analysis
Obtain tissue for culture, immunohistochemistry, and flow cytometry to differentiate between malignancy and chronic infection
